rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute allergic rhinitis with a patent airway and no anaphylaxis/asthma features is benign; manage at home with step-up therapy and routine GP review to assess the steroid spray (T1). The 'suffocating' sensation is nasal obstruction. A hard airway/anaphylaxis safety-net to T5 is mandatory.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Throat tightness or feeling of the throat closing, lip/tongue swelling, wheeze, difficulty swallowing, or true breathing difficulty
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates anaphylaxis/laryngeal oedema or bronchospasm — minutes-matter emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your throat feels tight or like it's closing, your lips or tongue swell, you wheeze, or you truly can't breathe — call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Development of high fever, productive purulent cough, or severe localized facial pain

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Reassure by explaining nasal vs airway breathing; provide explicit airway red flags.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Manage at home: daily steroid nasal spray, an antihistamine, avoid the dust, and a decongestant spray for no more than 3 days. Symptoms should ease within hours to days.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care with GP review in 1–2 weeks; 000 for any airway/anaphylaxis features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 for throat tightness/swelling/wheeze at any time.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Acute allergic rhinitis (dust trigger); airway/anaphylaxis screen negative; intranasal corticosteroid + antihistamine + avoidance.

critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving anaphylaxis with laryngeal oedema is dismissed as 'just hay fever' and the airway obstructs.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Not screening airway/wheeze before reassurance given the breathing-distress framing.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency referral/ambulance for benign allergic rhinitis with a patent airway — unnecessary.
